existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Perilunate Dislocation Operative Treatment

metadata.description: Operative management pathway for perilunate dislocation when nonoperative care is insufficient.

metadata.claim_text: Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for perilunate dislocation and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.
Document neurovascular status, key anatomic structures (lunate, capitate, scapholunate-ligament), stability assessment, and escalation plan for perilunate dislocation.
Missing lunate assessment in perilunate dislocation is a common board and OITE trap.
Treating perilunate dislocation in isolation without checking related wrist, tendon, or nerve neighborhoods in the cluster.
Early protected motion and therapy goals for perilunate dislocation should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.

metadata.action: Proceed with operative pathway when indicated; document fixation strategy, tendon/nerve protection, and postoperative plan
metadata.trigger: Unstable pattern, displacement, failed closed treatment, or functional deficit in perilunate dislocation

metadata.action: Splint/immobilize with therapy plan, repeat exam and imaging, and clear return precautions
metadata.trigger: Stable pattern with acceptable alignment and intact neurovascular exam for perilunate dislocation
